Paraguay shares similar red soils and flat landscapes with Brazil, particularly in the eastern regions. The vegetation and rural building styles are compatible, though slightly less diagnostic than the Brazilian interior.

The lowland tropical plains of Colombia can exhibit similar red soils and vegetation. However, the specific combination of the flat horizon and building style is more typical of the Southern Cone/Brazil.
The arid scrubland and red soil are consistent with the Sahelian landscape of Senegal. This serves as a cross-continent analog for the red-soil tropical savanna seen in South America.
